  "riskReason": "Logic change to support dual ability checking (ruler + grafana rules) introduces potential permission bypasses if either ability check fails silently.",

      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Logic Error - Permission Bypass",
      "file": "public/app/features/alerting/unified/components/rule-viewer/AlertRuleMenu.tsx",
      "line": 68,
      "title": "OR logic in ability checks may allow unintended permission bypass",
      "description": "The canPause, canDelete, canDuplicate, canSilence, and canExport logic now uses OR operators between ruler and grafana abilities (e.g., `(pauseSupported && pauseAllowed) || (grafanaPauseSupported && grafanaPauseAllowed)`). This means if one system reports an ability is allowed when it shouldn't be, the action becomes available. If skipToken is passed for non-grafana rules, grafanaAbilities will be [false, false] safely, but if there's any code path where both are evaluated with incorrect data, permission checks could be bypassed.",
      "suggestion": "Clarify the intended behavior: should an action be allowed only if BOTH systems permit it, or if EITHER permits it? If it should be AND, change to `(pauseSupported && pauseAllowed) && (grafanaPauseSupported && grafanaPauseAllowed)` or add explicit checks. If OR is correct, add comments explaining why dual-rule-type systems need permissive checks."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Type Safety - Missing Token Guard",
      "file": "public/app/features/alerting/unified/components/rule-viewer/AlertRuleMenu.tsx",
      "line": 64,
      "title": "Conditional skipToken logic may not prevent hook execution",
      "description": "The code passes `prometheusRuleType.grafana.rule(promRule) ? promRule : skipToken` to useGrafanaPromRuleAbilities(). While skipToken is intended to skip the query, if the hook doesn't properly handle skipToken (e.g., doesn't check for it before making requests), the ability check could still execute with undefined/null data, causing unexpected side effects or errors.",
      "suggestion": "Verify that useGrafanaPromRuleAbilities() hook properly checks for skipToken and short-circuits all query logic. If it doesn't, refactor to conditionally call the hook only when promRule is a grafana rule, rather than always calling it with a conditional argument."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Incomplete Test Coverage",
      "file": "public/app/features/alerting/unified/components/rules/RulesTable.test.tsx",
      "line": 24,
      "title": "Mock setup incomplete - mocks object doesn't match all imported hooks",
      "description": "The diff shows imports of useRulerRuleAbilities and useGrafanaPromRuleAbilities (plural forms), but the mocks object only defines mocks for the singular forms (useRulerRuleAbility, useGrafanaPromRuleAbility). When tests call the plural hooks, they will use the real implementation instead of mocks, potentially causing test flakiness or integration test failures.",
      "suggestion": "Ensure mocks object includes jest.mocked() entries for useRulerRuleAbilities and useGrafanaPromRuleAbilities (the plural versions that are actually imported and used by AlertRuleMenu)."
    }
  ]
